old_value: {"id":"feynfeld-43a.1","title":"Design Pair type system for Julia","description":"FeynCalc's Pair[x,y] encodes 3 things via argument heads: metric (LI,LI), four-vector (LI,Mom), scalar product (Mom,Mom). In Julia: decide whether to use one Pair type with canonical ordering + inner constructor (like FeynCalc) or separate types. Must handle Orderless (canonical arg ordering). Must integrate with TensorGR's TProduct. Research FeynCalc SharedObjects.m lines 1-300.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-26T11:01:41Z","created_by":"tobias","updated_at":"2026-03-26T11:01:41Z"}

new_value: Design spike complete: hybrid approach chosen (universal Pair type from FeynCalc + SPContext from Julia-idiomatic design + fold/reduce contract). Three agents ran: research, Design A, Design B.
Momentum type implemented in src/algebra/momentum.jl with DimSlot support, ordering, hash. 14 tests.
ScalarProduct mechanism implemented: Pair(Momentum,Momentum) replaces old ScalarProduct struct, SPContext for value storage, SP/SPD/SPE convenience constructors. 33 tests for Pair + 10 for SPContext.
{"assignee":"tobiasosborne","status":"in_progress"}
old_value: {"id":"feynfeld-43a.5","title":"Implement Contract (Lorentz index contraction)","description":"Port FeynCalc Contract. THE core operation. Algorithm: FCSplit to separate indexed/free parts → DummyIndexFreeQ to filter → tree-walk to find repeated indices → substitute. In FeynCalc uses PairContract trick (UpValues for eager contraction). In Julia: explicit tree-walking pass via TensorGR's contract_metrics or a dedicated contract() function. Must handle: metric contraction g^μν g_νρ, four-vector contraction p^μ g_μν, BMHV mixed-dim contractions. Ref: Lorentz/Contract.m (718 lines), PairContract.m (427 lines).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-26T11:01:43Z","created_by":"tobias","updated_at":"2026-03-26T11:01:43Z"}

new_value: Contract implemented in src/algebra/contract.jl: worklist reducer for metric-metric, metric-vector, vector-vector contractions. Full BMHV coverage (all 9x3 dim combinations). SPContext integration. Einstein violation detection. 46 contract tests.
{"assignee":"tobiasosborne","status":"in_progress"}
old_value: {"id":"feynfeld-43a.4","title":"Implement ExpandScalarProduct","description":"Port FeynCalc ExpandScalarProduct: expand Pair[Momentum[a+b], Momentum[c]] → Pair[Momentum[a],Momentum[c]] + Pair[Momentum[b],Momentum[c]]. Uses linearity, NOT Mathematica's Expand. Finds tensor objects containing Plus and expands individually. Ref: Lorentz/ExpandScalarProduct.m (important: does NOT use Mathematica Expand).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-26T11:01:43Z","created_by":"tobias","updated_at":"2026-03-26T11:01:43Z"}

old_value: {"id":"feynfeld-mpw.1","title":"Implement DiracGamma type with dimension tagging","description":"Port FeynCalc DiracGamma. Encodes: γ^μ (DiracGamma[LorentzIndex[μ]]), γ^5, γ^6=(1+γ^5)/2, γ^7=(1-γ^5)/2, slashed momenta (DiracGamma[Momentum[p]]). Dimension rules: DiracGamma[x,4]:=DiracGamma[x], DiracGamma[0,___]:=0, γ^5/6/7 always 4D, 4D object in (D-4) dims = 0. Mark as NonCommutative. Ref: SharedObjects.m DiracGamma section.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-26T11:02:27Z","created_by":"tobias","updated_at":"2026-03-26T11:02:27Z"}

old_value: {"id":"feynfeld-mpw.3","title":"Implement DiracTrick (core Dirac simplification rules)","description":"THE HARDEST FILE. Port FeynCalc DiracTrick.m (2102 lines). Core rules: γ^μ.γ_μ=D, γ^μ.γ^ν.γ_μ=(2-D)γ^ν, Clifford algebra {γ^μ,γ^ν}=2g^{μν}, commutation of γ^5/6/7 through chains, Dirac equation (p̸-m).u(p)=0, BMHV splitting. Uses sequence pattern matching (x___,γ^5,y___) → in Julia: explicit position-based iteration over chain elements. This will need multiple research rounds.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-26T11:02:28Z","created_by":"tobias","updated_at":"2026-03-26T11:02:28Z"}

new_value: DiracTrick core rules in dirac_trick.jl: gamma5 squared, projector algebra (g6/g7), slash squared, adjacent trace (g^mu g_mu = D), sandwich contraction n=1 (g^mu g^a g_mu = (2-D)g^a) and n=2. 35 new tests, 352 total.
{"assignee":"tobiasosborne","status":"in_progress"}
old_value: {"id":"feynfeld-mpw.5","title":"Implement DiracTrace (trace evaluation)","description":"Port FeynCalc DiracTrace.m (775 lines). Key: trace is a HEAD that wraps expressions, evaluated only when DiracTraceEvaluate→True. Without γ^5: standard recursive formula. With γ^5: scheme-dependent (NDR, BMHV, Larin). CRITICAL: Tr with single γ^5 in NDR is LEFT UNEVALUATED (genuine physical ambiguity). Must support 4 γ^5 schemes. TraceOfOne default=4. Ref: Dirac/DiracTrace.m.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-26T11:02:30Z","created_by":"tobias","updated_at":"2026-03-26T11:02:30Z"}

new_value: DiracTrace in dirac_trace.jl: recursive trace formula for non-chiral (Tr[g^a1...g^{2n}] = Σ (-1)^i g^{a1,ai} Tr[rest]), chiral base case (Tr[g5 g^a g^b g^c g^d] = -4 ε^{abcd}), g5 parity handling. 15 new tests, 367 total.

old_value: {"id":"feynfeld-sem.1","title":"Implement SU(N) type system (SUNT, SUNF, SUND, SUNDelta, indices)","description":"Port FeynCalc colour types. SUNT[a] = generator T^a (NonCommutative, lives in DOT chains). SUNTF[{a},i,j] = T^a with explicit fundamental indices. SUNF[a,b,c] = structure constants f^{abc}. SUND[a,b,c] = symmetric d^{abc} (Orderless). SUNDelta[a,b] = δ^{ab} adjoint. SUNFDelta[a,b] = δ^{ab} fundamental. SUNIndex[a] vs SUNFIndex[a]. CA=N, CF=(N²-1)/(2N). Ref: SUN/ types in SharedObjects.m.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-26T11:02:54Z","created_by":"tobias","updated_at":"2026-03-26T11:02:54Z"}

Implemented eps_contract.jl (~85 LOC): Eps·Eps → -det[pair(a_i,b_j)] via Leibniz formula. Added _indices(::Eps), _subst_factor(::Eps). Pre-pass in contract(). Verified: 4-shared=-24, 3-shared=-6g, 2-shared=2(gg-gg), momentum args correct.

Implemented gamma5 traces in dirac_trace.jl (158 LOC total). Projector expansion GA6→(1+γ5)/2, GA7→(1-γ5)/2. γ5 anticommutation + base case Tr[4γ+γ5]=-4ε. Recursive for n>4. All 9 MUnit validation targets pass. Zero regressions on 70 existing tests.

old_value: {"id":"feynfeld-e8d","title":"ARCH: Channel type and tree_channels() for 2→2 scattering","description":"Tree-level 2→2 scattering has exactly 3 topologies: s-channel, t-channel, u-channel. No graph enumeration needed. Design: (1) Channel struct with: topology (:s/:t/:u), internal field, vertex pair. (2) tree_channels(model, [in1, in2], [out1, out2]) → Vector{Channel} that enumerates valid channels by checking which internal particles can mediate each topology. Uses dispatch on model type. (3) For QED e+e-→μ+μ-: 1 s-channel (γ). For Bhabha: s+t channels (γ). For Compton: s+u channels (e). For qq̄→gg: s+t+u channels (g,q). Must handle: fermion flow direction, crossed channels, identical particles. ~60 LOC for Channel + tree_channels(). File: src/v2/channels.jl (new, \u003c100 LOC).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-29T11:29:09Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-29T11:29:09Z"}

old_value: {"id":"feynfeld-czy","title":"ARCH: build_amplitude() dispatch for Channel → DiracChain","description":"Given a Channel (topology + fields), construct the amplitude as DiracChain × AlgSum. Uses the existing FeynmanRules callable to get vertex factors and propagators, then assembles them into chains. Dispatch on channel topology: _build_s_channel(ch, rules) builds vertex-propagator-vertex. _build_t_channel analogous with crossed momenta. Must handle: (1) fermion propagator = i(p̸+m)/(p²-m²), (2) boson propagator = -ig^μν/(k²-M²), (3) vertex factors from rules(particles...), (4) correct fermion flow and index assignment. Depends on: Channel type (feynfeld-e8d). ~80 LOC. File: src/v2/amplitude.jl (new, \u003c100 LOC).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-29T11:29:21Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-29T11:29:21Z"}

They validate physics but don't test the architecture. Actions: (1) Add a comment block at top of each file: '# REFERENCE IMPLEMENTATION — validates physics formula directly. # The pipeline should reproduce this result via Model→Rules→Diagrams→Algebra→Evaluate.' (2) For each, once the pipeline can handle the process, add a pipeline test that cross-checks against the standalone result. This is a documentation + integration task, not a rewrite.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-29T11:29:50Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-29T11:29:50Z"}

{"notes":"DEFERRED: Native Julia reimplementation of ffxc0 is premature. The FF library is 3,474 lines of battle-tested Fortran with 30 years of numerical stability engineering. Strategy change: use LoopTools.jl ccall for C₀ NOW, reimplement in native Julia as a long-term project when we understand every edge case."}
old_value: {"id":"feynfeld-0x0","title":"Implement FF library C₀ (ffxc0): correct analytical C₀ for all kinematic regions","description":"The Denner C0p3 formula (C0func.F) is a backup double-checker in LoopTools, not the primary algorithm. It fails for certain kinematic regions (at/above threshold, degenerate momenta). The primary LoopTools algorithm is the FF library (ffxc0.F by van Oldenborgh), which uses a single-pass Spence decomposition without the 3-channel split and handles ALL cases correctly. Translate ffxc0.F (~500 LOC Fortran) to Julia. This replaces the current threshold-check workaround and quadgk fallback.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-29T17:55:20Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-29T17:55:20Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"DEFERRED: η correction bug is moot once we use LoopTools.jl ccall. The Denner C0p3 formula (C0func.F) is a backup checker, not the primary algorithm. Investigating its η issues is low priority while we have the robust FF library via ccall."}
old_value: {"id":"feynfeld-tiv","title":"Implement correct η-function corrections for Denner Spence decomposition","description":"The current c0_analytical.jl omits the 't Hooft-Veltman η corrections (Eq 2.4) because the Denner C0p3 formula gives wrong results with them for spacelike kinematics. Root cause unknown — the Julia code matches the Fortran line-by-line but produces different numerical results. Either: (a) find the subtle bug in the η implementation, or (b) replace with FF library which avoids the issue entirely. This blocks correct analytical C₀ above threshold.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-29T17:55:22Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-29T17:55:22Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"RESOLVED BY STRATEGY CHANGE: C0p2 a≈0 degenerate case is handled correctly by the FF library (ffxc0). Using LoopTools.jl ccall eliminates this issue."}
old_value: {"id":"feynfeld-ioa","title":"Fix C0p2 degenerate case: a≈0 when p10≈p20 (vertex correction kinematics)","description":"The Denner C0p2 formula divides by a = px1-px2 (or px3-px1), which is zero when two nonzero momenta are equal. This is the standard QED vertex correction kinematics (p10=p20=m², p12=q²). Currently falls back to slow quadgk. Fix: either derive the L'Hôpital limit for a→0, or use the FF library (ffxc0) which doesn't have this singularity. Blocked by the FF library implementation if we want a clean fix.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-29T17:55:21Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-29T17:55:21Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"SUPERSEDED: Threshold workaround becomes unnecessary once we use LoopTools.jl ccall for all non-C0p0 cases."}
old_value: {"id":"feynfeld-3ze","title":"Remove threshold-check workaround once FF library C₀ is implemented","description":"c0_analytical.jl currently uses _C0_above_threshold() to fall back to quadgk for at/above-threshold kinematics. This is correct but slow. Once the FF library (ffxc0) is implemented, remove the threshold check and route all C₀ through analytical evaluation. Also remove the result==0 degenerate-case fallback.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-29T17:55:24Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-29T17:55:24Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"PARTIALLY COMPLETE: C0p0 (all momenta zero) is analytically solved. For general C₀, switching to LoopTools.jl ccall (FF library). Native Julia analytical C₀ deferred to future work."}
old_value: {"id":"feynfeld-fqy","title":"PERF: Analytical C₀ via 't Hooft-Veltman Spence formulas (replace nested quadgk)","description":"Current C₀ uses nested quadgk (1-5ms per call, 1000x slower than necessary). The 't Hooft-Veltman 1979 analytical formula gives C₀ in terms of 2-4 dilogarithm (Li₂) evaluations — ~1-10μs. LoopTools (refs/LoopTools/src/C/) implements this with case dispatch: C0p0 (all momenta zero, pure logs), C0p1/C0p2/C0p3 (1/2/3 nonzero momenta, Spence formulas), C0soft/C0coll (IR/collinear limits). Julia already has PolyLog.jl with li2. Port the analytical formulas, dispatch on kinematic case. ~200-400 LOC. This alone would cut test_vertex_g2 from 8min to \u003c1sec. Ref: refs/papers/tHooftVeltman1979_NuclPhysB153.pdf Eqs (5.2)-(5.30). Ref: refs/LoopTools/src/C/C0func.F.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"tobias.osborne@itp.uni-hannover.de","created_at":"2026-03-29T13:55:55Z","created_by":"tobiasosborne","updated_at":"2026-03-29T13:55:55Z"}
